feature_build: requested/effective model capture, verifier policy metadata, and cost accounting

source_provenance.claim: Adapter policies validate role and effort before provider invocation.
source_provenance.verification: tests/contracts/loop-run-contract.test.js rejects unsupported adapter roles and effort levels before invocation.

source_provenance.claim: Claude and Codex adapter commands use requested_model while preserving fallback_model where supported.
source_provenance.verification: tests/contracts/loop-run-contract.test.js checks --model for Codex and --fallback-model for Claude.

source_provenance.claim: Provider event parsing extracts effective model, fallback/refusal reason, usage, token counts, and estimated cost when exposed by provider output.
source_provenance.verification: tests/contracts/loop-run-contract.test.js parses JSONL with model, fallback reason, usage, and cost fields.

source_provenance.claim: Adapter ledger entries preserve requested model and effective model separately, including a Fable-class request routed to an Opus-class effective model.
source_provenance.verification: tests/contracts/loop-run-contract.test.js uses the contract-allowed fake provider to simulate requested claude-fable-5 with effective claude-opus-4-8 and re-reads ledger entry fields.

source_provenance.claim: Run status summarizes model/cost counters without inventing missing usage or effective-model metadata.
source_provenance.verification: tests/contracts/loop-run-contract.test.js asserts requested/effective model counters, unknown usage entries, known cost, and cost per gate pass.

source_provenance.claim: Installed safe adapter policies expose planner and implementer roles for fresh projects.
source_provenance.verification: tests/contracts/loop-run-contract.test.js validates normalized role/effort fields and command construction against the same adapter policy schema.
